page: Google Cloud Platform a2-highgpu-8g is an accelerator-optimized server configured with 8 NVIDIA Ampere A100 GPUs providing a total of 320 GB of VRAM. The system is powered by an Intel Xeon x86_64 processor featuring 96 dedicated vCPUs (48 physical cores with hyper-threading) running at 2.2 GHz, supported by 680.0 GB of system memory. No local storage is bundled with this instance type.

Benchmark data reveals a clear performance tradeoff. While single-core CPU metrics, such as stress-ng and Geekbench single-core scores, fall into the bottom 25%, multi-core performance is exceptionally strong, placing in the top 25% for stress-ng multi-core and PassMark CPU tests. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, with read-only cached speeds reaching 4215.1 GB/sec. Crucially, the server excels in LLM inference, achieving top-tier (top 10%) performance in prompt processing across small, medium, and large models, as well as text generation for 7B and 70B models.

Given the high density of hardware accelerators and massive system memory, this server offers a specialized cost-efficiency profile tailored for resource-intensive workloads rather than general-purpose computing. The absence of local storage requires external storage provisioning. It is highly suited for deep learning, large language model (LLM) inference, high-performance computing (HPC), and parallelized data science tasks.
description: Google Cloud Platform a2-highgpu-8g is an accelerator-optimized server designed for massive parallel workloads. It features 8 NVIDIA Ampere A100 GPUs with 320 GB of total VRAM, paired with 96 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs (48 physical cores) running at 2.2 GHz and 680.0 GB of system memory. This instance does not include local storage. Performance benchmarks highlight a significant tradeoff: single-core CPU performance is relatively weak, whereas multi-core execution and memory bandwidth are exceptionally strong. Most notably, the server delivers top-tier performance for large language model inference across various model sizes. This hardware profile is highly efficient for specialized, GPU-intensive tasks, making it best suited for deep learning, LLM inference, and high-performance computing.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform a2-highgpu-8g is an accelerator-optimized server featuring 8 NVIDIA Ampere A100 GPUs, 96 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, and 680 GB of RAM, designed for high-performance multi-core and AI workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform a2-highgpu-8g features 8 NVIDIA A100 GPUs, 96 Intel Xeon vCPUs, and 680 GB RAM, delivering top-tier performance for LLM inference.
tagline: An accelerator-optimized platform featuring eight Ampere GPUs and massive memory bandwidth for high-performance parallel computing and language model inference.
bullet_points: Equipped with 8 NVIDIA Ampere A100 GPUs providing 320 GB of VRAM
Features 96 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 680 GB of system memory
Delivers top-tier performance for large language model (LLM) inference
Strong multi-core CPU and high-speed memory bandwidth capabilities
No bundled local storage, requiring external storage attachment
Optimized for deep learning, parallel data science, and HPC workloads
